data.title: Fitch Ratings: Outlook Neutral for U.S. Healthcare Providers in 2026
data.description:
Related Content: U.S. Healthcare Providers Outlook 2026

Fitch Ratings-New York-11 December 2025: Fitch Ratings’ ‘neutral’ Outlook for the U.S. Healthcare Providers sector for 2026 calls for mid-single-digit revenue growth, driven by low-single-digit growth in volumes, low-to-mid-single-digit mix-adjusted growth in reimbursement rates, and M&A likely rising amid further sector consolidation. Given issuer resilience and sector stability, Fitch expects balanced rating actions in the sector in 2026, with most ratings likely stable.

Fitch sees EBITDA margins flattening in 2026 after meaningful recent improvement, reflecting salary, wages and benefits likely rising at mid-single digit rates as tailwinds from reducing temporary staffing costs have dissipated and with medical professional fees likely rising by about 10%. Fitch is monitoring the level of pressure on margins from adverse shifts in payor mix with the uninsured population likely to grow in 2026.

The 2025 Tax Cuts & Jobs Act tightens Medicaid eligibility and will drive a more rigorous annual redetermination process in 2026, which Fitch expects to reduce Medicaid enrollment, leaving more of the population uninsured. Pressure from the 2025 Act will step up in 4Q27, when reductions in federal matching funds tied to non-compliant provider tax structures take effect. While the effects of the 2025 Act are clearly negative for providers, Fitch expects they will be manageable for most, especially near term.

U.S. Healthcare Providers will also see some expansion of site neutral payment policies in 2026, as certain off-campus

hospital outpatient departments reimbursed under Medicare’s Outpatient Prospective Payment System (OPPS) rule will begin receiving payments for drug administration at only 40% of the OPPS rate received previously. The 2026 Medicare OPPS rule also phases out the inpatient-only list by YE 2027, sustaining the ongoing migration of surgical cases from hospital inpatient settings to less costly outpatient settings. This latest move is likely to sustain top line and margin tailwinds for ambulatory surgery centers (ASCs) at hospitals’ expense.

The full report is available at www.fitchratings.com or by clicking the link above.

Todd Corsair

Senior Director

+1-212-908-0505

todd.corsair@fitchratings.com

Fitch Ratings, Inc.

33 Whitehall Street, New York, NY 10004

Media Relations: Eleis Brennan, New York, Tel: +1 646 582 3666, Email: eleis.brennan@thefitchgroup.com

Additional information is available on www.fitchratings.com

Read More On This Topic

data.title: Brent should average $65/bbl in 2026, Barclays says
data.description:
Brent crude oil is expected to average $65 per barrel next year, Barclays said on Thursday, while projecting a supply surplus for the year.

"We expect a 1.9 million barrels per day surplus next year, but inventories remain low and builds continue to surprise to the downside," Barclays said in a note. It added that spare capacity is low and geopolitical tensions continue to pose asymmetric upside risks to prices.

Barclays highlighted that a potential slowdown in cyclical demand remains a key downside risk. However, given its outlook for non-OPEC+ supply, the bank noted that OPEC+ could respond to weakening fundamentals in such a scenario.

The OPEC+ group comprising the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries, Russia and other allies plans to pause production hikes in the first quarter of 2026, amid widespread predictions of oversupply.

World oil supply will match demand closely in 2026, OPEC data published earlier in the day indicated, an outlook contrasting with projections from the International Energy Agency and others of a huge glut.

On the geopolitical front, the U.S. is preparing to intercept more ships transporting Venezuelan oil following the seizure of a tanker this week, sources familiar with the matter said.

Brent crude futures and U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude settled lower for the day.

data.title: Lululemon to Increase Marketing Spend in 4Q to Drive Traffic, Build Brand Awareness, CFO Says
data.description: This news item displays a headline only and has no other text.

data.title: Lululemon Stock Pops on Earnings Beat and CEO Change — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Sabrina Escobar

Lululemon Athletica stock jumped Thursday after the company posted a better-than-expected fiscal third quarter and said CEO Calvin McDonald would be stepping down next year.

The company announced that McDonald plans to step down on Jan. 31. The board of directors has yet to name a permanent successor. Chief Financial Officer Meghan Frank and Chief Commercial Officer André Maestrini will serve as interim co-CEOs until then.

The CEO change comes as Lululemon continues to struggle with building up demand in the Americas, the company's largest market.

Although total comparable-store sales increased 1% from a year ago — in line with projections — comparable-store sales in the Americas fell 5% from a year ago.

Lululemon's slowing sales trends have been weighing heavily on the stock this year, with shares down a little over 50%. Shares were up 5.3% in after-hours trading.

Lululemon's third-quarter performance helped, as did the board's approval of a $1 billion increase to the company's stock repurchase program.

Revenue rose 7% year over year to $2.6 billion, topping analysts' projections for $2.48 billion. Adjusted earnings of $2.59 a share were also ahead of estimates calling for $2.21 a share, according to FactSet.

Lululemon slightly increased its guidance for the fiscal year. It now sees net sales ranging from $10.962 billion to $11.047 billion, slightly higher than the prior range calling for $10.85 billion to $11 billion.

Earnings per share will be between $12.92 to $13.02, up from the prior range of $12.77 to $12.97 for the year.

Analysts are expecting adjusted earnings of $12.85 a share on $10.96 billion in revenue.

This is breaking news. Check back soon for more updates.

Write to Sabrina Escobar at sabrina.escobar@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Rivian Unveils Custom Chips, New AI Models And Autonomy Subscription To Power Self-Driving Push
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - Rivian Automotive revealed major advances in its self-driving strategy at its first 'Autonomy and AI Day' on Thursday, showcasing custom chips, a new vehicle computer and proprietary AI models that will support autonomous capabilities in its next-generation vehicles.

Shares fell as much as 9 percent during the event, partly pressured by OpenAI's own AI announcement.

The company said it will launch an Autonomy plus subscription for its second-generation vehicles starting in early 2026, priced at USD 2,500 upfront or USD 49.99 per month.

The service will be powered by Rivian's new Rivian Autonomy Processors and autonomy computers. By comparison, Tesla's premium supervised FSD package costs USD 8,000 or USD 99 per month.

Rivian plans to use lidar and radar in its upcoming R2 vehicles to eventually reach Level 4 automation, a standard associated with fully automated driving.

Scaringe said this capability also positions Rivian to pursue future robotaxi opportunities, expanding beyond personal vehicle ownership.

Copyright(c) 2025 RTTNews.com. All Rights Reserved

Copyright RTT News/dpa-AFX

data.title: Vext Announces Voting Results of Annual General Meeting
data.description:
Vancouver, British Columbia--(Newsfile Corp. - December 11, 2025) - Vext Science, Inc.  (OTCQX: VEXTF) ("VEXT" or the "Company"), a U.S.-based cannabis operator with vertical operations in Arizona and Ohio, today announced final voting results from its annual general meeting of shareholders held on December 11, 2025 in Vancouver, British Columbia (the "Meeting").

The shareholders of the Company voted in favour of all matters included in the management information circular for the Meeting, demonstrating strong support for the matters brought before the Meeting, with each of the resolutions passing with over 97% of the votes cast, representing 74% of the votes associated with the issued and outstanding shares of the Company.1

For more details, visit Vext's investor website or contact the IR team at investors@vextscience.com.

About VEXT Science, Inc.

Vext Science, Inc. is a U.S.-based cannabis operator with vertical operations in Arizona and Ohio. Vext's expertise spans from cultivation through to retail operations in its key markets. Based out of Arizona, Vext owns and operates state-of-the-art cultivation facilities, fully built-out manufacturing facilities as well as dispensaries in both Arizona and Ohio. The Company manufactures Vapen™, one of the leading THC concentrates, edibles, and distillate cartridge brands in Arizona. Its selection of award-winning products are created with Vext's in-house, high-quality flower and distributed across Arizona and Ohio. Vext's leadership team brings a proven track record of building and operating profitable multi-state operations. The Company's primary focus is to continue growing in its core states of Arizona and Ohio, bringing together cutting-edge science, manufacturing, and marketing to provide a reliable and valuable customer experience while generating shareholder value.

Vext Science, Inc. is listed on the Canadian Securities Exchange under the symbol VEXT and trades on the OTCQX market under the symbol VEXTF. Learn more at www.vextscience.com and connect with Vext on Twitter/X and LinkedIn.

For more details on the Vapen brand:

Vapen website: VapenBrands.com

Instagram: @vapen

Facebook: @vapenbrands

The Canadian Securities Exchange has not reviewed, approved or disapproved the content of this news release.

Eric Offenberger

Chief Executive Officer

844-211-3725

For further information:

Jonathan Ross, Vext Investor Relations

jon.ross@loderockadvisors.com

416-244-9851

SOURCE: Vext Science, Inc.

1In total, 183,712,741 shares out of 247,683,573 shares the Company has issued and outstanding, were voted at the Meeting. The reference to "shares" means the Company's common shares and Class A shares reported on an as converted basis.

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/277810

data.title: Fitch Affirms WakeMed, NC at 'A+'; Outlook Stable
data.description:
Fitch Ratings-New York-11 December 2025:

Fitch Ratings has affirmed WakeMed Health & Hospital's (WakeMed) Long-Term Issuer Default Rating (IDR) at 'A+' and the ratings on revenue bonds issued directly by WakeMed or by the Public Finance Authority of Wisconsin on behalf of WakeMed at 'A+'.

The Rating Outlook is Stable.

The 'A+' rating considers WakeMed's leading market position in a very favorable service area and Fitch's expectation that its balance sheet metrics will strengthen over the medium term. The Stable Outlook reflects Fitch's view that the system's operating margins will continue to improve and be maintained at levels that are consistent with a 'strong' operating risk assessment.

SECURITY

The bonds are secured by an interest in pledged assets.

KEY RATING DRIVERS Revenue Defensibility - 'bbb'

Leader in Favorable, Competitive Service Area

WakeMed is the market leader in a favorable service area despite the presence of notable competitors. In its primary service area (PSA), the system captured a nearly 41% inpatient market share in FY 2025, followed by UNC Health with just under 37% and Duke Health (IDR: AA-) with 14%. In Wake County alone, the system held a 46.4% inpatient market share in FY 2025; UNC Health and Duke Health held 34.1% and 17.4%, respectively. In addition to competing against these rival health systems, WakeMed maintains partnerships with both Duke Health and UNC Health, along with other regional health networks.

Duke Health has a hospital in Wake County (Duke Raleigh Hospital) and is planning a major ambulatory investment in the county (which may eventually include an inpatient hospital). Despite the elevated degree of local competition, Fitch believes WakeMed is positioned to maintain its leading market share given its own sizable investments in the service area. Fitch notes that robust population growth across the PSA and the broader region should support volume capture by all acute care providers active in the market.

WakeMed's payor mix is in line with a midrange assessment, despite the system being classified as Wake County's safety net hospital. The ratio of combined Medicaid and self-pay is consistently in the 21%-22% range (21.2% in FY 2025 unaudited).

Following North Carolina's recent Medicaid expansion, Fitch anticipates that the ratio of combined Medicaid to self-pay should remain steady, but Medicaid will make up a greater proportion of the total. This has been evidenced by Medicaid's share of the total increasing from 14.5% of gross revenues in FY 2023 to 16.9% in FY 2025, while self-pay decreased from 6.3% of gross revenues to 4.3%. Wake County and the broader region benefit from very favorable demographic indicators that include robust population growth and below average unemployment rates.

Operating Risk - 'a'

Significant Rebound in FY 2025 Brought Margins Back to Pre-2022 Levels

Operating margins have improved significantly following a substantial operating loss in FY 2022. Margins improved significantly in FY 2023 and FY 2024 (adjusted to include receipt of FEMA funds), with strong operating momentum sustained through FY 2025 (unaudited), driven primarily by volume gains in most key areas and further reductions in contract labor and staff turnover. Fitch expects the system to achieve an operating EBITDA margin of approximately 9% - similar to FY 2025 - in FY 2026. WakeMed's operating EBITDA margins averaged around 9% between FY 2017 and FY 2021; FY 2025 results therefore represent a return to pre-pandemic norms.

Notable volume gains in FY 2025 included inpatient admissions (up 6.8% over FY 2024), hospital stays (up 4.2%), unique patients (up 7.4%), and births (up 5.6%). Inpatient and outpatient surgical volumes also rebounded strongly (up 5.5% and 5.9%, respectively) after declining in FY 2024 due to a change in anesthesiology providers. Outpatient visits (up 22.9%), however, were the biggest outperformer, as new care sites drew in higher volumes.

Capital Spending Plans Will Ramp Up in 2026 and 2027

WakeMed has a strong track record of consistent capital reinvestment, which is consistent with a system that has grown market share in a population growth area. The capital spending ratio averaged just over 100% between FY 2020 and FY 2024, and the average age of plant measured 14.5 years at FYE25.

Between FY 2026 and FY 2030, WakeMed has $723 million in total capex spending planned. Current and future spending is focused on improving access, including adding new MyCare 365 primary and urgent care sites (there are currently 13 MyCare 365 locations, with additional sites planned), a new linear accelerator, and additional bed capacity at both North Hospital and Cary Hospital.

The highlighted big-ticket future capital project is construction of a new whole health campus in Garner, in south Wake County. The Garner campus will span 56 acres and is expected to include two new hospitals: a 45-bed acute care hospital with 24 ED rooms and a 150-bed mental health and well-being hospital with behavioral health beds and outpatient services. The total estimated cost for the project is $400 million, which management plans to fund with approximately $250 million to $350 million of additional debt (factored into the rating), county, state and federal government grants and approximately $50 million in philanthropic proceeds.

Management has already received state certificate of need (CON) approval for the Garner campus and is currently targeting the start of construction for late 2026 with the opening of the campus planned for late 2028.

Financial Profile - 'a'

Financial Profile Consistent with Current Rating, Assuming Operating Results Continue to Improve

Fitch expects that WakeMed's capital-related balance sheet metrics will improve, even in a stress scenario, particularly given improved cash flow. At unaudited FYE25 unrestricted cash and investments improved to about $998 million from $797 million at FYE24. As a result, cash-to-debt increased to nearly 106% at FYE25 from 87% at FYE24 (factoring in $100 million in three hybrid leases).

WakeMed's adjusted debt is equivalent to its direct debt because operating leases are captured on the balance sheet and its frozen defined benefit (DB) pension plan is well over 100% funded.

As the result of improved operating margins and better cash flow generation, liquidity growth is likely to follow, and Fitch expects WakeMed's debt-related metrics to rebound to levels more in line with an 'A+' rating by the out years of our scenario analysis, even under a stress scenario. In our Fitch's five-year stress case, WakeMed's net adjusted debt-to-adjusted EBITDA would return to a favorably negative position by year four, while cash-to-adjusted debt never falls below 76% and would reach approximately 133% by year five.

Liquidity is not an asymmetric risk to the financial profile as cash on hand measured 150 days at FYE25. Likewise, maximum annual debt service (MADS) coverage is not an asymmetric risk, measuring 6.6x in FY 2025 (unaudited). WakeMed's debt is all fixed rate.

RATING SENSITIVITIES Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Negative Rating Action/Downgrade

--Failure to sustain meaningful rebound in operating metrics, especially if the operating EBITDA falls back below the 7%-8% range;

--Faltering balance sheet momentum, particularly if cash-to-adjusted debt fails to improve to levels that would allow for it to be sustained above 100% in the out-years of Fitch's forward-looking stress case.

Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Positive Rating Action/Upgrade

--Upward rating action is not expected during the outlook period;

--Longer term, a rating upgrade might be considered if WakeMed's cash-to-adjusted debt shows further improvement and was expected to remain above 120% even under a forward-looking stress case;

--Sustained rebound in operating metrics such that operating EBITDA margins are expected to remain in the 9% range.

PROFILE

WakeMed is an integrated health system headquartered in Raleigh, NC with more than 175 sites of care, primarily located in Wake County. The system includes three acute care hospitals and a 73-bed rehab hospital. WakeMed operates the only two trauma capable hospitals in Wake County: WakeMed Raleigh (Level I) and WakeMed Cary (Level III).

The system recorded more than $2.6 billion of operating revenue in unaudited FY 2025, representing significant top-line revenue growth since 2020 (total operating revenue grew by about 75% between FY 2020 and FY 2025). WakeMed is a private not-for-profit health system and Wake County no longer owns the system's facilities; however, the majority of WakeMed's board is appointed by the county.

REFERENCES FOR SUBSTANTIALLY MATERIAL SOURCE CITED AS KEY DRIVER OF RATING The principal sources of information used in the analysis are described in the Applicable Criteria. ESG Considerations

The highest level of ESG credit relevance is a score of '3', unless otherwise disclosed in this section. A score of '3' means ESG issues are credit-neutral or have only a minimal credit impact on the entity, either due to their nature or the way in which they are being managed by the entity. Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores are not inputs in the rating process; they are an observation on the relevance and materiality of ESG factors in the rating decision. For more information on Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores, visit https://www.fitchratings.com/topics/esg/products#esg-relevance-scores.

WakeMed Health & Hospitals (NC); Long Term Issuer Default Rating; Affirmed; A+; Rating Outlook Stable

----WakeMed Health & Hospitals (NC) /General Revenues/1 LT; Long Term Rating; Affirmed; A+; Rating Outlook Stable

data.title: Kinsale Capital Group Authorizes $250M Stock Buyback
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Kinsale Capital Group will buyback up to $250 million of it stock under a new share repurchase program approved by its board of directors.

The authorization follows completion of a $100 million buyback program, the specialty insurance company said Thursday.

"Today's announcement reflects our confidence in Kinsale's future and the value we see in our stock," Chief Executive Officer Michael Kehoe said.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Fortune Brands Raises Dividend 4%
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Fortune Brands Innovations raised its quarterly cash dividend by 4% to 26 cents a share.

The new dividend is 1 cent higher and will be payable on March 11, 2026, to shareholders of record as of Feb. 20, 2026, the maker of home fixtures said.

The new payout, equal to $1.04 a year, represents an annual yield of 2.0%, based on Thursday's closing price of $52.44.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com
